examples: Schoenberg used atonality in the music of his middle period.
His basic musical idioms were based on the tradition of tonality, but he also, if necessary, resorted to dissonances and occasional atonality.
Idea and imagination are reciprocal causation. On the on hand, an abstract music image is created by the atonality and complex playing skills.
Yet the progression toward atonality also had a mystical aspect: these uncanny new chords could serve as esoteric icons, emblems of the sacred.
